content:
💡 youthub | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: Watching YouTube videos directly from the terminal without using a web browser.
✨ Key Features: • TUI grid with previews • Custom ffplay-yt player • SponsorBlock integration • 1080p video playback • Direct SABR streaming
📖 Summary: YouHub is a terminal-based YouTube client that allows users to browse, search, and watch YouTube videos directly from their console. It features a TUI grid interface, a custom ffplay-yt player, and integrates features like SponsorBlock for an ad-free experience without needing a web browser.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🌟 DeadReckoning | Java
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide accurate and reliable location tracking on Android devices, especially in environments where GPS is not available or functional.
✨ Key Features: • Real-time localization • Uses inertial sensors (accelerometer, compass, gyro) • GPS-independent tracking • Offline functionality • Self-sufficient location tracking
📖 Summary: Dead Reckoning is an Android application that provides real-time localization using a phone's inertial sensors, eliminating the need for GPS. It enables self-sufficient and offline location tracking, useful in environments where GPS signals are unreliable or unavailable.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

✨ branch-output | C++
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide advanced, multi-output streaming and recording capabilities for OBS Studio, allowing users to send individual sources or scenes to different destinations with custom configurations.
✨ Key Features: • Individual streaming and recording per source/scene
📖 Summary: Branch Output is an OBS Studio plugin that enhances streaming and recording capabilities by allowing individual sources or scenes to be configured with dedicated encoding settings and audio sources. It provides advanced features like automatic reconnection, per-filter replay buffers, and manual file splitting, offering granular control over output streams.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🌟 Claude-Code-Game-Studios | Shell
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide structure and organization to AI-assisted game development within a single Claude Code session, enabling a more efficient and professional workflow.
✨ Key Features: • 49 specialized AI agents organized into a studio hierarchy (Directors, Department Leads, Specialists) • 73 workflow skills (slash commands) for all phases of game development
📖 Summary: Claude Code Game Studios transforms a single Claude Code session into a structured, multi-agent game development studio. It organizes AI agents into a hierarchy with specialized roles and skills, mirroring a real studio's workflow to improve project organization, catch errors early, and maintain project vision.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 GenericAgent | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: Enabling Large Language Models (LLMs) to autonomously control and interact with local computer systems, automating tasks and evolving capabilities over time.
✨ Key Features: • Self-Evolving: Automatically crystallizes tasks into reusable Skills, growing a personal skill tree. • Minimal Architecture: Core code is ~3K lines, Agent Loop is ~100 lines, with no complex dependencies.
📖 Summary: GenericAgent is a Python framework for creating self-evolving autonomous AI agents. It allows LLMs to control local computer systems through a minimal set of tools and an agent loop, automatically learning and growing its capabilities into a personal skill tree with each task it completes.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source
